humanTouchpoints.label: Weekly coaching check-in
humanTouchpoints.description: 15-minute call with Dr. Reyes to review the week's glucose log, troubleshoot blockers, and adjust the plan.

humanTouchpoints.label: Lab review
humanTouchpoints.description: Baseline and end-of-program lab draw (fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid panel) reviewed together to quantify progress.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Stabilize
phases.description: Stop the spikes and start measuring. The goal of the first four weeks is to remove the biggest blood-sugar drivers (liquid sugar, refined-carb breakfasts) and build the daily glucose-logging habit so every later decision is data-driven.

milestones.title: Baseline & daily monitoring
milestones.summary: Get a clear starting picture and make morning glucose logging automatic.

tasks.title: Get baseline labs (fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid panel)
Log fasting blood glucose first thing each morning
Drink 16 oz of water before breakfast

milestones.title: Remove refined sugar
milestones.summary: Cut the fastest blood-sugar spikers first: sugary drinks and refined-carb breakfasts.

tasks.title: No sugar-sweetened drinks (swap for water or sparkling water)
Protein-first breakfast, no added sugar or refined carbs
Plan and prep low-glycemic meals for the week
tasks.actionPrompt: Build a 7-day low-glycemic meal plan for a client reversing type 2 diabetes, using their food preferences. Protein + fiber + healthy fat at every meal, under 30g net carbs per meal. Include a grocery list.

milestones.title: Move every day
milestones.summary: Add a small, repeatable dose of movement that blunts post-meal glucose.

tasks.title: 10-minute walk after dinner
Weigh in (same scale, Monday morning)

phases.title: Phase 2 — Rebuild
phases.description: Now that the spikes are under control, build metabolic flexibility: low-glycemic plates, a consistent eating window, strength training, and the sleep and stress habits that quietly drive insulin resistance.

milestones.title: Low-glycemic eating
milestones.summary: Make every plate blood-sugar friendly and tighten the eating window.

tasks.title: Build each plate: protein + fiber + healthy fat, eat carbs last
Eat within a 10-hour window (time-restricted eating)
Continue logging fasting blood glucose each morning

milestones.title: Strength + zone-2 cardio
milestones.summary: Muscle is a glucose sink. Alternate resistance work with easy aerobic days.

tasks.title: 20-minute resistance / bodyweight strength session
30-minute brisk walk or zone-2 cardio

milestones.title: Sleep & stress
milestones.summary: Protect sleep and downshift stress — both raise fasting glucose when neglected.

tasks.title: Lights out by 10:30pm (target 7.5+ hours sleep)
5-minute breathing / stress downshift before bed
Weekly coaching check-in call with Dr. Reyes

phases.title: Phase 3 — Sustain
phases.description: Lock in the wins. Reintroduce a few foods while watching the glucose response, cement the habits that moved the numbers, and re-test labs to measure how far blood sugar has come.

milestones.title: Reintroduce & test
milestones.summary: Test tolerance for a few foods using glucose as the referee.

tasks.title: Reintroduce one food and log the 2-hour glucose response
Continue morning fasting glucose log

milestones.title: Cement the habits
milestones.summary: Keep the movement and eating patterns that did the work.

tasks.title: Hit daily step goal (8,000+ steps)
Maintain eating window + protein-first plates
Weigh in (Monday morning)

milestones.title: Re-test & plan forward
milestones.summary: Measure the result and build the maintenance plan.

tasks.title: Repeat labs (fasting glucose, HbA1c) to measure progress
Maintenance plan review call with Dr. Reyes
